marketOverview: Global markets navigating complex landscape of geopolitical tension and shifting monetary policy expectations. Key focus remains on US economic data and central bank rhetoric.

currencies.name: US Dollar
currencies.rationale: USD supported by warsh Fed Chair nomination (hawkish, criticizes forward guidance), ISM Manufacturing expansion (52.9, vs exp 50.3), and Michigan Sentiment rebound (77.4). Strong US data, including hotter Core PCE print at 3.0% YoY, combined with Fed Miran's hawkish pivot from 150bps to 100bps of expected cuts have firmed US front end yields. Externally, tariff escalation and elevated geopolitics keep defensive demand elevated, supporting carry advantage.

currencies.name: Euro
currencies.rationale: EUR under pressure from disinflation (Eurozone 1.7%, Core 2.2% below target) and dovish ECB signal despite Rate Hold at 2%. German Manufacturing PMI surged to 50.7 (first expansion in 3.5 years), but EUR could not sustain gains as higher US two year yields and more hawkish Fed tone reasserted dollar support. Lagarde succession speculation adds political risk discount, limiting relative rate channel that previously helped Euro. Trade escalation under Section 122 reintroduces export sensitivity for bloc still reliant on external demand.
drivers: German Manufacturing PMI 50.7 (first expansion in 3.5 years)
ECB Negotiated Wages Q4 3.0% (slightly above exp)
EU sanctions package against Russia stalled

currencies.name: British Pound
currencies.rationale: GBP volatile after BoE dovish surprise (5-4 split for Rate Cut to 4.5%, Governor Bailey expects 'quite sharp' inflation drop). Markets price April cut. Political crisis (Starmer leadership challenge over Mandelson appointment) adds risk premium. Manufacturing PMI 51.8 and resilient housing data offset by policy shift. Structural weakness dominates near-term outlook.
drivers: BoE Governor Bailey expects 'quite sharp' inflation drop

currencies.name: Japanese Yen
currencies.rationale: JPY weakest G10 currency as election-related messaging (Iwaki landslide election victory) and wide yield differentials drive carry trades. BoJ remains patient (April hike odds 60%, but messaging weak JPY messaging, surprising market). Downside surprise in Q4 GDP and softer January CPI print (headline 1.5% vs 1.6% exp) reduced conviction. Tokyo CPI tests 1.5%, GBP/JPY highest since 2008. USD/JPY tests 157, GBP/JPY highest since 2008. Lower House Elections Sunday critical for policy path.
drivers: January CPI ex Food Energy 2.5% (vs 2.6% exp)
FOMC Minutes confirmed USD/JPY rate checks

currencies.name: Australian Dollar
currencies.rationale: AUD supported by RBA hawkish surprise (Rate Hike to 4.85%, further hikes possible if wages/inflation persist) and global risk-on rally (Dow >8k). US Critical Minerals Stockpile initiative boosts terms of trade. Copper volatility and China growth concerns remain, but domestic rates impulse dominates. High-beta cyclical benefits from equity strength.
drivers: Three Month LME Copper trending higher

currencies.name: Canadian Dollar
currencies.rationale: CAD resilient but vulnerable to trade risks (Trump warns of 'substantial response' if Canada proceeds with China deal). Jobs Report mixed (unemployment 6.5%, Manufacturing PMI expansion). BoC halts cuts (Rate Hold at 2.25%), but +40k full-time, Unemployment 6.5%, Manufacturing PMI expansion. BoC halts cuts (Rate Hold at 2.25%), GDP flash 0.2% YoY. Oil volatility (±0.5%) provides limited support. WTI strength linked to US SPR. Headlines around potential rethink of USMCA structure in favor of bilateral deals reinforced Canada's external vulnerability.

currencies.name: Swiss Franc
currencies.rationale: CHF as fear gauge: Mid-week safe-haven bid (Tech sell-off, Iran-up geopolitical tensions) quickly faded as Friday risk-on rally (Dow 50k) drove capital rotation. Negative rates only as high-bar tool. Carry disadvantage dominates when risk sentiment stable. Soft Q4 GDP at 0.2% (vs 0.3% exp) and contracting Q4 Industrial Production at -0.7% reinforce weak structural backdrop. SNB zunehmend dovish (CHF-Stärke, niedrige Inflation).
drivers: Swiss Domestic Bank Sight Deposits rose to CHF 519bn
SNB policy outlook: negative rates only as high-bar tool

currencies.name: New Zealand Dollar
currencies.rationale: NZD Boom-Bust: Rally auf Kupfer-ATH (USD 15,530) und China-Nvidia-approval, dann Reversal auf Metall-Crash (Gold -10%, Silver -30%). Hotter Inflation begrenzt RBNZ-Easing. By signaling that OCR is likely to remain at current levels in near term and only potentially rise in late 2026 if inflation proves stickier than expected, Governor Breman has effectively removed near term tightening premium. China/Australia linkage remains critical for exports.
drivers: RBNZ dovish pivot under Governor Breman
OCR to remain at current levels near term
Potential rise late 2026 if inflation sticky
